artifacts.content:
Mami,

I’m finally sitting down for a minute now that Sofia’s in bed. I wanted to tell you what’s been going on because it’s all piling up.

The noise here at 1842 Willow Creek Drive is getting worse. You know how my bedroom in Apt 3B shares a wall with Evan Thompson’s living room in 3A? On the night of 1/15/26 around 11:45 PM his music was so loud the wall was vibrating. Sofia woke up having a full-blown panic attack that kicked off her asthma. I had to give her an emergency albuterol treatment like Dr. Patel at Riverton Family Clinic showed us, and I almost took her to the Riverton Community Hospital ER. This isn’t the first time either – he had parties on 12/20, 12/31, 1/3, and 1/10 that all went past 1–2 AM.

The lack of sleep is killing me at work. At Riverton Family Clinic I’m already on a performance improvement plan from those billing errors back in October 2025, and Melissa Grant gave me an informal attendance warning on 1/12 after I missed two early shifts in December because of these late nights. I’m scared one more mistake and I’ll lose the job and the health insurance.

On top of that I’m still trying to keep up with the $3,200 Poudre Valley Hospital bill for your surgery in Greeley. I put it on a three‑month plan, but I already missed the 1/15 payment because I had to cut back overtime hours from being so exhausted.

I know you’re dealing with your own health stuff, but I just needed to tell someone who understands. Sofia keeps asking if we can move because she’s afraid when she hears the yelling through the wall. I’m trying to put away even $25 at a time, but it feels impossible.

Love you,
Jennifer

metadata.subject: Things here are getting really hard

artifacts.content:
Hi Karen,

I wanted to follow up after the last couple of rough weeks because I’m worried about how things are affecting my work at Riverton Family Clinic.

As you know, I’ve been on the performance improvement plan since October 2025 for billing errors. The late-night disturbances at my apartment at 1842 Willow Creek Drive (Apt 3B) have made it really hard to get consistent sleep. On 10/2/25, for example, Evan next door cranked his music so loud around 11:45 PM that my bedroom wall was shaking while I was trying to correct that $3,200 Medicaid billing error Angela wrote me up for back in August. More recently, the parties on 12/20, 12/31, 1/3, 1/10, and 1/17 have left me so exhausted that I’ve been double‑ and triple‑checking simple codes.

You probably saw my 1:27 AM email on 10/13/25 when I said I couldn’t make my 8:00 AM shift because of a migraine after another night of noise. I hate having to do that, especially while I’m already on the 90‑day attendance performance plan from when I missed days earlier in the year while my mom, Elena, was hospitalized at UCHealth Greeley Hospital for her diabetes complications.

I’m doing everything I can on my end: logging in remotely after Sofia goes to bed to catch up on overdue claims (I still remember staring at the 17 leftover claims from the week of 12/30/24 at 12:05 AM), using earplugs, white‑noise apps, coffee – probably too much coffee.

I just wanted you to have the full picture and to reassure you I’m taking the PIP seriously. If there are any adjustments we can make to help me stay on track (different shift start time, fewer late‑night batches), I’m open to ideas.

Thank you,
Jennifer Morales
Medical Billing Specialist

metadata.subject: Context about recent performance and attendance

artifacts.content:
Personal journal – 2025-10-13

I feel like everything is fraying at once.

Last night (technically early this morning) the hallway outside 3B was chaos again. Evan’s people were running up and down, shouting. It reminded me too much of Miguel’s drunk outburst outside Building C back on November 3, 2025 in Greeley, and even further back to his domestic violence arrest in Aurora in March 2022 and the DUI mess on May 6, 2021. My body reacts before my brain does – heart racing, hands shaking, like I’m back in that parking lot.

Around 12:30 AM I picked up my phone to call the courtesy officer, then froze thinking about the Riverton PD report from March 2023 and the protection order file at Larimer County. I still remember how that almost messed up my lease renewal. I’m terrified of being seen as a “problem tenant” if there are more police calls tied to my name or to 1842 Willow Creek.

Miguel keeps hinting that if Sofia keeps being woken up, he’ll use it in the Arapahoe County custody review that’s running from September 2025 through March 2026. Lisa Hernandez warned me in her Denver office last November that anything tied to this address could show up in Case No. 21DR-1184 at the February 2025 hearing.

So I sit here with my spiral notebook instead, logging dates and times of the noise on the same pages where I’m tracking the $1,275 I still owe Poudre Valley REA from the shut‑off notice on 1/10/24 and the $300 Mom loaned me. Noise, bills, court, all tangled together.

I have therapy with Dr. Karen Lopez at Riverton Community Behavioral Health tomorrow at 6:30 pm. I need to tell her the panic attacks feel like they did right after Miguel’s DUI.

Event: Sofia – school / counseling / pediatric follow-ups

Date: 2025-12-12 (and ongoing reminders)
Time: 3:30 PM
Location: Riverton Children’s Clinic / Riverton Elementary / various

Notes:
- 12/12/25 – Appointment with Dr. Patel at Riverton Children’s Clinic for Sofia. Reason: panic attacks and almost no sleep (less than 4 hours/night) after three nights of Mr. Thompson’s parties coming through the wall in Apt 3B. He adjusted her anxiety meds and reminded me to track her sleep.
- Add reminder: email Ms. Harper and Ms. Kim at Riverton Elementary about why Sofia may be tired/jumpy in class and behind on assignments, especially after the October 3–5, 2025 noise and the bad night on 9/14/25 when neighbors were slamming doors past midnight.
- Weekly since November 2025 – counseling with school social worker Laura Jenkins for anxiety tied to loud arguments she used to hear between me and Miguel. After the 1/17/26 party when she woke up screaming around 12:45 a.m., I emailed Ms. Jenkins on 1/18 to ask for an extra session.
- Ongoing: Riverton Pediatric Behavioral Health check‑ins with Dr. Patel (behavioral) about emergency anti‑anxiety dose we used on 10/12/25 after Sofia’s panic attack at 12:45 AM from hallway traffic; she missed Riverton Elementary the next day.
- Remember to bring notes from Riverton Child & Family Counseling (Dec 4, 2025 follow‑up after Miguel showed up drunk on 12/3) and Riverton Children’s Counseling Center (night terror call with Dr. Willis after 11/14/25 music).

Also: schedule pediatric follow‑up at Poudre Valley Hospital in Fort Collins for her recurring stomach issues (used 4 unscheduled sick days in May–July 2025).

Subject: Catching you up on the family circus

Hey Carlos,

Writing this on my lunch break at Riverton Family Clinic because if I don’t vent, I’m going to explode.

First, thank you again for spotting me that $200 back in August after the fight next door on 8/22/25. Skipping the $95 Capital One payment on 8/18 so I could buy those noise‑cancelling headphones and the white‑noise machine felt irresponsible, but honestly they’re the only reason Sofia and I sleep at all in 3B. I’m still chipping away at the $1,275 I owe Poudre Valley REA from those December 2023–March 2024 bills and the shut‑off notice on 1/10/24, plus paying you back.

Work is shaky. Between the upstairs people and Evan in 3A, I’ve already been late twice this month. Melissa Grant put a note in my file after our 12/19/25 check‑in about me looking exhausted and making more billing mistakes. Remember the 8/27/25 Medicare batch Karen Lopez wrote me up for after the TV and subwoofer were going past 1 AM? It’s like that again. I can’t afford to lose this job or the insurance, not with Mom’s heart procedure at UCHealth Poudre Valley Hospital and the $275/month payment plan I set up in September 2025.

Miguel isn’t helping. After the neighbors in 4B were screaming on 11/27/25, I ended up on the phone with him from like 1:30 to 2:30 a.m. and now he’s talking about revisiting custody because he says this place is “unsafe.” Meanwhile, Sofia’s the one waking up crying and wetting the bed.

Anyway, how’s Denver? Are you still going to group at Denver Recovery Center? If you feel up to it, maybe you could come up one weekend and take Sofia to the park so I can sleep.

Love,
Jen

metadata.subject: Catching you up on the family circus

artifacts.content:
Thread with Ana (close friend)

Ana: You alive? Haven’t heard from you in forever.

Me: Barely. Willow Creek is still a disaster.

Me: On 1/10/26 there was this insane party in 4C. Around 12:15 a.m. glass shattered right outside my door at 3B, Sofia woke up having a full‑blown panic attack. We ended up sitting on the bathroom floor for almost an hour doing the breathing Ms. Ramirez taught her at Riverton Elementary after that fight with Miguel back in Aurora.

Ana: Oh my God. Poor Sofi. Did you call the cops?

Me: No. I wanted to, but ever since officers came to the old Aurora apartment in 2021 and Sofia saw Miguel in handcuffs during that DV mess, I promised her I’d only call if someone’s life was in danger.

Me: Instead I was on the phone with Melissa (my supervisor) earlier that week, literally standing in the courtyard by Building B on 1/10/25 crying and describing what Miguel was doing during another blow‑up. After the October 2024 write‑up when he violated the temporary restraining order and I missed work, I’m terrified of anything that looks like “drama.”

Ana: This is so much. Are you still seeing Dr. Harding?

Me: Yeah, telehealth through Riverside Mental Health Clinic in Fort Collins. I even had to cancel a 7:00 AM session on 1/11/25 because Evan’s party the night before went past 2 and my anxiety was through the roof.

Me: Honestly, between that and the 1/6/25 parking‑lot argument behind Building C when Miguel was yelling about custody in front of Sofia, I feel like I live in a trigger factory.

Work/health planning note – 2025-12-03

Today’s appointment with Dr. Karen Patel at Riverton Community Health Clinic was a wake‑up call. She officially noted in my chart that my anxiety has gotten worse since the upstairs unit started with the constant late‑night noise in early November. She increased my sertraline from 50 mg to 100 mg and even said I might have to think about short‑term disability if my job at Riverton Family Clinic keeps being affected.

I hate that idea because we’re already short on money. I’m behind $275 on December rent thanks to unexpected co‑pays earlier this month, plus the past‑due $145 utility bill from June and Sofia’s $60 co‑pay from her June 10, 2025 pediatric appointment with Dr. Patel in Greeley. I was counting on about $180 in overtime this summer, but I canceled two evenings after that 2 a.m. party on 7/20/25 because I was too wiped out.

Add in Mom’s health: her heart procedure at UCHealth Poudre Valley Hospital and the $275/month payment plan from September, her chemo days at UCHealth in Greeley, and the blood pressure scare when she stayed over on 1/10/26 and her reading spiked to 182/104 after 20 minutes of hallway yelling (had to call the North Colorado Medical Center nurse line and note it for Dr. Patel the cardiologist).

I need this job, including the ~$230/month employee health insurance subsidy Amanda warned me about losing at that 9/19/24 HR meeting when I got written up for being late three times in a month.

Action items:
- Keep sleep log + noise log for next 4 weeks.
- Ask Melissa about any flexibility with early shifts.
- Keep up therapy at Riverton Behavioral / Community Behavioral Health.

Notes to self:

- Home Cell Number: (303) 555-4829
- Business Phone: (720) 555-1934
- Unit If Known 2: Apt 3A
- Names Of Other Witnesses To The Incident: Melissa Carter
- Unit If Known Witness: Apt 4B
- Day Of Month: 22.0
- Month: January
- Year: 2026.0
- Resident Signature: Jennifer L. Morales
